claim: Responses API unifies completions, chat, tools, and file/web search into one endpoint; deprecates Assistants API by mid-2026
actionable: Migrate from Assistants API to Responses API now; Responses API is the new standard for agents and tool calling
source_title: OpenAI - NEW API & Agent Tools Breakdown

claim: OpenAI web search tool costs $0.03-$0.05 per call depending on context size (high context ~$0.05); returns links embedded in text, not structured fields
actionable: Use web search for agents needing real-time data; parse links from returned text manually; high context size justified for most agentic tasks
source_title: OpenAI - NEW API & Agent Tools Breakdown

claim: File Search: updated RAG tool with vector store, metadata filtering, citation tracking; limited to 100GB; pricing includes storage plus retrieval costs
actionable: Use for quick RAG without external vector DB; test citation extraction; monitor storage costs alongside retrieval
source_title: OpenAI - NEW API & Agent Tools Breakdown

claim: Computer Use model provides coordinates for clicks/typing given screenshot + command; OpenAI provides no cloud browser—integration requires Docker or Playwright locally
actionable: Build your own browser sandbox (Docker/Playwright) or use third-party cloud providers; OpenAI only sells the model, not the execution environment
source_title: OpenAI - NEW API & Agent Tools Breakdown

claim: Computer Use pricing: $12/million output tokens is reasonable; human oversight still recommended as model can run unintended actions (use credit cards, passwords)
actionable: Never give computer use agent access to sensitive credentials; implement approval gates and logging; plan for hallucinations and error recovery
source_title: OpenAI - NEW API & Agent Tools Breakdown
